what_it_does: This file argues that review/annotation work should be organized into structured 'passes' (developmental, line, copy/proof) rather than treated as a flat stream of individual marks, and it proposes making the pass a first-class node with its own focus, status boundary, and pre-submit summary UI.
observations: Defines professional review as structured rounds or passes, not a single stream of marks.
Lists pass types: developmental pass, line pass, and copy/proof pass.
Reframes Tim's 'make a dozen, submit them all' batch (BD-F) as a review pass rather than a delivery convenience.
States a pass should have a focus that is shared with the supervisor and members as reading context.
Describes a pass-level note as a mark on the whole pass, not on any single block.
Positions the pass as the natural resolution boundary and rollup for per-mark status.
Maps the pass concept to design-map's 'session concept for an annotation pass' (BD-K INTENT-LEVEL).
Proposes the submit screen become a pass summary grouped by verb (change, query, note, approve), each tappable to its anchor.
References dragnet run hinge-node pattern (A7 step 2) as a reusable structural shape.
File is a noticeboard block in the dragnet-development channel and links as part_of board://item-a72b23c2.
standouts: Reframing a batch submit as a professional review pass.
Making the pass a first-class node that marks hang off.
The submit screen as a pass-summary ritual grouped by verb/anchor.
conventions: Uses editorial/legal review terminology (developmental pass, line pass, copy/proof pass, markup rounds).
References existing brief atoms by shorthand (BD-F, BD-D ADDITIONS, BD-K INTENT-LEVEL, A7 step 2).
Follows the noticeboard item format with YAML metadata and a markdown body.
Proposes a UI/UX convention (pre-send pass summary) without specifying implementation.
concerns: Blends product concept, process advice, and UI convention without clear separation.
Assumes familiarity with referenced brief atoms that are not defined in this file.
Does not specify a concrete data model or schema for the proposed pass node.
notes: A design argument that review submissions should be treated as structured 'passes' with their own focus, status, and a pre-send summary UI.
questions: What is the exact data-model relationship between a pass and a run hinge-node?
How are pass types represented and validated in the system?
Does the proposed pass-summary screen replace or augment the existing 'send all' button?
What are the specific per-mark statuses referenced by BD-D ADDITIONS?
How is pass focus captured and displayed to recipients?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose—arguing that review is structured passes and proposing pass-first design—matches the content.
apparent_themes: First-class containers for collections of annotations.
Professional editorial and legal review workflows.
Reusing structural shapes across subsystems (pass, run hinge-node, annotation session).
intention_signals: It is trying to elevate the review pass from a UI convenience to a core workflow/data-model concept and to align design-map annotation sessions with dragnet run-node patterns.
novelty: Reframing a 'send all' batch as a professional 'pass' with its own focus, top-level note, resolution boundary, and pre-send summary ritual.
contribution: The concept of a pass as a first-class container for review marks with a stated focus, a resolution boundary, and a rollup status; plus the convention of a pre-submit pass summary grouped by verb and anchor.
summary_for_embedding: This noticeboard item argues that review marks should be organized into structured 'passes' (developmental, line, copy/proof) with a focus, a pass-level note, a resolution boundary, and a pre-submit summary grouped by verb.
intention_for_embedding: It was written to propose elevating the batch/submit workflow into a first-class review-pass node and aligning it with the design-map annotation session and dragnet run-hinge patterns.
